mnemonics: Knapsack: Keep Valuable Items, Never Adding Stuff Past Allowed Capacity & Keep
Memoize: Memorize Old Optimal Values, Eliminating Repetition In Zero/One Evaluations
keyPoints: The 0/1 Knapsack problem involves items that cannot be split; you either take the whole item or leave it.
Dynamic Programming solves the problem by building a table (dp[i][w]) where each entry represents the maximum value achievable with the first 'i' items and a weight limit 'w'.
Memory Functions (the memoization technique) implement a top-down approach that recursively solves subproblems, stores their results in a table, and reuses these results to avoid recomputation.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly initializing the DP table, especially the first row (no items) and first column (zero capacity).
Confusing the 0/1 Knapsack (discrete items) with the Fractional Knapsack (items can be split), which is solved with a greedy algorithm.
